concept: Intervene / Deliberately Act on a Health State or Care Process
description: To act deliberately on a health state or a care process, more broadly than 'nephoru' (treat): diagnostic, preventive, supportive, and palliative acts all qualify. The exact procedure, when one has a name, stays in the source record beside the Phi.
sound_symbolism: Attentive 'the' approaches a decisive 'ka', while final 'mu' places the action within a bounded body or care setting. The form marks deliberate entry without treating it as justified.
grammatical_notes: A transitive or intransitive verb: 'shia ha thekamu phoa.' (They perform this intervention.) The event-noun rule supplies an intervention. State the action, person, purpose, source, consent, risks, alternatives, and follow-up where relevant. Distinct from 'nephoru' (treat), 'somaki' (test), 'palo' (touch), 'theama' (care), and a source-specific procedure.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A neutral intervention word makes bodily action discussable without building benefit, legitimacy, professional authority, or consent into the verb.

tags.activity: a deliberate action within bodily care
tags.community: an action affecting a person within a care relation
tags.physical: an action on a body, health state, or bodily environment
